title: invoice — subprocessors
description: The Invoice module engages TWO categories of third-party subprocessors: (1) PDF storage + delivery (AWS S3 for hot storage, S3 Glacier for cold archive), and (2) Enterprise-only collections agency integrations (per-tenant configurable — this file lists the platform-supported agencies). GDPR Art. 28 DPA references for each. The tenant admin acknowledges the DPA before configuring a collections agency; PDF storage is transparent (Academorix's infrastructure vendor, not an active engagement per tenant).

subprocessors.purpose: Object storage for invoice + credit note PDFs. Hot storage in S3 Standard; migration to S3 Glacier at retention boundary (7 years default, 10 for Enterprise).
data_categories: invoice PDF binaries (containing customer billing_address, invoice line details, tax breakdown, totals)
subprocessors.data_hashing: N/A — PDF content is stored encrypted at rest (SSE-KMS) but not hashed. Client-side encryption not applied.
sub_processing_locations: configurable per tenant — us-east-1 default; EU tenants may pin to eu-west-1

subprocessors.purpose: Cold archive for invoice + credit note PDFs after the hot retention window. Regulator-facing retention; retrieval requires 3-12h thaw.
Server-side encryption key management for S3 PDF storage. SSE-KMS with tenant-specific data keys (envelope encryption).

Third-party collections agency handoff for past-due invoices. Enterprise-only. Only FDCPA-compliant (US) / EU-consumer-credit-directive-compliant (EU) licensed agencies may be configured. Notifications module owns the actual integration; this module dispatches the handoff.
subprocessors.data_hashing: none — collections agencies need the raw customer contact info to pursue the debt
subprocessors.dpa_url: per-agency — tenant admin acknowledges each agency's DPA before configuring
certifications: per-agency: FDCPA license (US), Financial Conduct Authority (UK), etc.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges the specific agency's DPA + FDCPA/regulatory license reference before configuring; Enterprise entitlement invoice_collections_integration required

subprocessors.purpose: Delivery transport for invoice email (SendGrid, Postmark, SES, custom SMTP). Owned by the notifications module; this file references it because invoice emails carry PDFs + PII.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Email transport for invoice emails + push transport for InvoiceDueSoonNotification + collections integrations.

Payment tokens (Stripe, Paddle) reference the payment module's subprocessor list. Invoicing does not directly touch payment providers — the MarkPaidOnPaymentIntentConfirmed listener reads confirmed intent state from the payment module.
Tax calculation providers (Avalara, TaxJar, Stripe Tax) referenced when the tax module resolves TaxCalculation for invoice lines.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.reference: compliance::encryption owns the AWS KMS key hierarchy used for SSE-KMS on invoice PDF storage.

notes: This file exists deliberately with 2 categories of active subprocessors. AWS S3 + KMS are platform-level (Academorix's infrastructure — not a per-tenant engagement). Collections agencies are per-tenant + Enterprise-only.

The tenant-facing subprocessor list (compliance/subprocessors surface) reads from this file + shows only the active subset per tenant (based on which collections agency the tenant has configured, if any).

Adding a new collections agency = adding a new entry to a data catalogue (data/collections-agency-catalog.json — TBD) + updating the notifications module's integration + updating compliance.json Art. 28 evidence.

PDF storage on S3 is protected by SSE-KMS encryption at rest — even AWS staff cannot decrypt without the tenant-specific KMS keys. Encryption keys are rotated per compliance/encryption cadence (default 90 days).

Collections agencies receive raw customer PII (name, address, phone, email) — this is regulator-mandated (FDCPA requires the agency to be able to contact the customer). The tenant admin explicitly opts-in per agency.
